title: DirectX & Command Buffers
description: Understand Microsoft DirectX graphics APIs (DX11 vs DX12), command queues, and descriptor heaps.
learningObjectives: Differentiate high-level DX11 abstractions from low-level DX12 control pipelines
Record rendering instructions into Command Lists and submit them to Command Queues
Manage GPU descriptor resources using Descriptor Heaps

sections.title: Modern Low-Level API Pipelines
sections.content:
Microsoft's DirectX APIs represent the graphics standard on Windows and Xbox. While DirectX 11 uses a single immediate context to execute commands sequentially, DirectX 12 moves to a low-level, multi-threaded model. 

In DX12, you record draw instructions into multiple independent `Command Lists` concurrently across CPU cores, and submit them to a centralized `Command Queue` (the GPU work coordinator). This reduces CPU driver bottlenecks significantly but requires manual memory barrier synchronization (resource transitioning).

sections.title: DirectX 12 Command Submission
